title: Is WordPress dead? (Or just too stubborn to notice?)
summary: WordPress is becoming obsolete for modern web development, suffering from systemic performance problems—fewer than half of WordPress sites pass Core Web Vitals on mobile. While the plugin ecosystem was its strength, the 97% of vulnerabilities originating from plugins now make maintenance a liability rather than an asset.
content:
I've been building and maintaining WordPress sites for years. Clients, side projects, quick landing pages — WordPress was always the default answer. Need a website? WordPress. Need a blog? WordPress. Need a portfolio? WordPress with a theme and three plugins.
And for a long time, that made sense. WordPress powered a massive share of the web, had a plugin for everything, and you could hand it off to a non-technical client who could update their own content. The ecosystem was unmatched.
But lately, every time I spin up a new WordPress instance, I can't shake the feeling that I'm solving a 2026 problem with a 2006 tool. And I don't think I'm alone.
Let's start with the thing nobody wants to say out loud: WordPress is slow.
Not theoretically slow. Not "if you don't optimize it" slow. Slow by default. A fresh WordPress install with a commercial theme and a handful of essential plugins — contact form, SEO, caching, security — already ships more PHP, more database queries, and more JavaScript than most sites actually need.
And here's the painful number: fewer than half of WordPress sites on mobile pass Google's Core Web Vitals. For a platform that powers over 42% of the web, that's not a minor issue — that's a systemic design problem.
The response from the WordPress community is always the same: use a caching plugin, optimize your images, pick a lightweight theme, configure your CDN. In other words, spend hours undoing the bloat that WordPress introduced in the first place. You're not building a website at that point. You're performing surgery on one.
WordPress's greatest strength — its plugin ecosystem — has quietly become its biggest liability.
There are over 70,000 plugins available. That sounds impressive until you realize that up to 97% of WordPress security vulnerabilities come from plugins. Every plugin you install is a dependency you now have to maintain, update, and trust. And while most site owners do keep WordPress itself updated, the plugin ecosystem is where t

zh.title: WordPress已死吗？（或者只是太固执而没有注意到？）
zh.summary: WordPress对于现代网络开发来说正在变得过时，受到系统性性能问题的困扰——少于一半的WordPress网站在移动设备上通过Core Web Vitals。虽然插件生态曾是它的优势，但来自插件的97%的漏洞现在使维护成为负债而不是资产。

fr.title: WordPress est-il mort ? (Ou trop têtu pour le remarquer ?)
fr.summary: WordPress devient obsolète pour le développement web moderne, souffrant de problèmes de performance systémiques - moins de la moitié des sites WordPress réussissent Core Web Vitals sur mobile. Bien que l'écosystème des plugins soit sa force, les 97% des vulnérabilités provenant des plugins font maintenant de la maintenance une responsabilité plutôt qu'un atout.

de.title: Ist WordPress tot? (Oder einfach zu stur, um es zu bemerken?)
de.summary: WordPress wird für moderne Webentwicklung zunehmend obsolet und leidet unter systematischen Leistungsproblemen - weniger als die Hälfte der WordPress-Websites bestehen Core Web Vitals auf Mobilgeräten. Während das Plugin-Ökosystem seine Stärke war, macht die 97% der Sicherheitslücken, die von Plugins stammen, die Wartung nun eher zu einer Verbindlichkeit als zu einem Vorteil.

es.title: ¿Está WordPress muerto? (¿O simplemente demasiado terco para notarlo?)
es.summary: WordPress se está volviendo obsoleto para el desarrollo web moderno, sufriendo de problemas de rendimiento sistémicos - menos de la mitad de los sitios WordPress aprueban Core Web Vitals en dispositivos móviles. Aunque el ecosistema de complementos fue su fortaleza, el 97% de las vulnerabilidades originarias de complementos ahora hacen que el mantenimiento sea una responsabilidad en lugar de un activo.

title: Etiqueta <html>: El Universo de tu Web
summary: The HTML root element contains the entire web page and requires strategic attributes like `lang` for accessibility and SEO. Setting CSS variables on `:root` ensures they're available globally, making it the proper foundation for scalable, accessible web architecture.
content:
Si tu sitio web fuera un organismo vivo, la etiqueta <html> sería la piel que lo contiene todo. Es el elemento raíz (root) y absolutamente nada en tu página puede existir fuera de sus fronteras.
El navegador utiliza esta etiqueta para saber dónde empieza y dónde termina el documento. Es el ancestro común de todos los elementos (el "padre de todos"). 
Un desarrollador novato deja la etiqueta vacía: <html>. Un Arquitecto Web como tu utilizas los atributos estratégicos:
 lang="es": (Obligatorio para Seniors) Le dice a los motores de búsqueda y a los lectores de pantalla en qué idioma está el contenido. Evita que el navegador pregunte "¿Quieres traducir esta página?".
 dir="ltr": Define la dirección del texto (de izquierda a derecha). Fundamental si algún día trabajas con idiomas como el árabe (rtl).
<html lang="es">
  <head>
    </head>
  <body>
    </body>
</html>

En CSS, puedes seleccionar este elemento usando el selector :root. Es la mejor práctica para declarar tus Variables CSS (colores, tamaños, fuentes) porque garantiza que estarán disponibles para absolutamente todos los elementos de tu sitio.

zh.title: HTML标签：你的Web宇宙
zh.summary: HTML根元素包含整个网页，需要战略性属性如`lang`以实现可访问性和SEO。在`:root`上设置CSS变量确保它们全局可用，使其成为可扩展、可访问网页架构的适当基础。

fr.title: Balise <html> : L'univers de votre web
fr.summary: L'élément racine HTML contient la page web entière et nécessite des attributs stratégiques comme `lang` pour l'accessibilité et le référencement. La définition de variables CSS sur `:root` garantit qu'elles sont disponibles globalement, ce qui en fait la base appropriée pour une architecture web évolutive et accessible.

de.title: HTML-Tag: Das Universum deines Webs
de.summary: Das HTML-Wurzelelement enthält die gesamte Webseite und erfordert strategische Attribute wie `lang` für Barrierefreiheit und SEO. Das Festlegen von CSS-Variablen auf `:root` stellt sicher, dass sie global verfügbar sind, was es zur richtigen Grundlage für skalierbare, barrierefreie Webarchitektur macht.

es.title: Etiqueta <html>: El Universo de tu Web
es.summary: El elemento raíz HTML contiene toda la página web y requiere atributos estratégicos como `lang` para accesibilidad y SEO. Establecer variables CSS en `:root` garantiza que estén disponibles globalmente, lo que la convierte en la base adecuada para una arquitectura web escalable y accesible.

title: Day 23: Agentic AI in Product Management 🧠📦
summary: Agentic AI augments product managers by continuously monitoring user feedback, usage data, and market signals—surfacing patterns and trade-offs humans miss. The PM remains the decision-maker; agents accelerate synthesis, not strategy.
content:
Executive Summary


Product Management is fundamentally an information synthesis problem.
PMs constantly:
ingest noisy signals 🗣️

balance competing constraints ⚖️

make decisions with incomplete data ❓



Agentic AI does not replace product managers.
It augments them by:
continuously sensing inputs

structuring ambiguity

accelerating decision cycles



This chapter explains how agentic systems can be embedded into real product workflows without turning PMs into passive reviewers.
Product work involves:
open-ended questions

multi-stakeholder inputs

iterative refinement



These are classic agent traits:
Observe → Interpret → Decide → Act → Learn

Static dashboards fail because product decisions are:
contextual

temporal

value-laden



Agents thrive where spreadsheets break.
monitor signals continuously 📊

surface trade-offs explicitly

summarize user feedback at scale

propose options, not answers




  
  
  What They Should NOT Do



decide product direction autonomously ❌

override PM judgment

optimize for vanity metrics



Product strategy is a human responsibility.



Signal Type
Examples




User feedback
Tickets, NPS, surveys


Usage data
Funnels, cohorts


Market intel
Competitor launches


Engineering
Velocity, incidents


Business
Revenue, churn



Agents unify signals humans rarely see together.
Signals (Users, Data, Market)
            ↓
     Ingestion Agents
            ↓
     Normalization Layer
            ↓
     Insight Agents
            ↓
     Trade-off Analyzer
            ↓
     PM Review & Decision

The PM remains the decision-maker.
Thousands of feedback items across:
tickets

app reviews

sales notes




  
  
  Agent Behavior



cluster feedback by theme

track sentiment over time

flag emerging issues




  
  
  Outcome


PMs see patterns, not anecdotes.
“If we delay Feature X by one quarter, what breaks?”
scan dependencies

evaluate customer commitments

estimate revenue risk



This turns gut feel into structured debate.
Agents can

zh.title: 第23天：产品管理中的代理AI 🧠📦
zh.summary: 代理AI通过持续监控用户反馈、使用数据和市场信号来增强产品经理的能力——发现人类遗漏的模式和权衡。产品经理仍是决策者；代理加速综合分析，而非战略制定。

fr.title: Jour 23 : L'IA agentive en gestion de produit 🧠📦
fr.summary: L'IA agentive augmente les chefs de produit en surveillant continuellement les commentaires des utilisateurs, les données d'utilisation et les signaux du marché - en mettant en évidence les modèles et les compromis que les humains manquent. Le PM reste le décideur ; les agents accélèrent la synthèse, pas la stratégie.

de.title: Tag 23: Agentic AI im Produktmanagement 🧠📦
de.summary: Agentic AI unterstützt Produktmanager, indem es kontinuierlich Benutzer-Feedback, Nutzungsdaten und Marktsignale überwacht - und Muster und Kompromisse aufdeckt, die Menschen übersehen. Der PM bleibt Entscheidungsträger; Agenten beschleunigen die Synthese, nicht die Strategie.

es.title: Día 23: IA Agente en Gestión de Productos 🧠📦
es.summary: La IA agentiva augmenta a los gerentes de producto al monitorear continuamente los comentarios de los usuarios, datos de uso y señales del mercado, revelando patrones y compensaciones que los humanos pierden. El PM sigue siendo el tomador de decisiones; los agentes aceleran la síntesis, no la estrategia.

title: How I Fixed a Race Condition in a Live Seat Booking System (And Lost Sleep Over It)
summary: A race condition in a seat-booking system exposed a critical concurrency flaw: read-then-write operations are never atomic. The fix collapses reading and booking into a single atomic MongoDB operation, with Socket.io broadcasts ensuring instant client-side consistency.
content:
𝗧𝘄𝗼 𝘂𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘀. 𝗢𝗻𝗲 𝘀𝗲𝗮𝘁. 𝗧𝗼𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗼𝘀.
My first instinct? "That can't happen — I have a check before booking." Famous last words.
User A and User B both queried the seat at nearly the same millisecond. Both saw it as available. Both proceeded to book. The database updated twice — same seat, two owners.
I tried a simple if check before updating — reading the seat status, then writing. Seemed logical. Broke immediately under any real concurrency. Read-then-write is not atomic. The window between those two operations is exactly where race conditions live.
Then I tried a client-side flag. Also wrong. Socket.io events can arrive out of order, and the UI state is not a source of truth.
The solution was to collapse the read and the write into a single atomic MongoDB operation — and pair it with a Socket.io broadcast so every connected client sees the lock instantly.
async function reserveSeat(tripId, seatNo, userId) {
// Prevents Race Conditions
if (!trip) throw new Error("Seat already taken!");
// Broadcast the 'Lock' to all travelers
Seat ${seatNo} secured for User ${userId});
1️⃣ 𝙏𝙝𝙚 𝙦𝙪𝙚𝙧𝙮 𝙘𝙤𝙣𝙙𝙞𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣 𝙞𝙨 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙜𝙪𝙖𝙧𝙙
2️⃣ 𝙛𝙞𝙣𝙙𝙊𝙣𝙚𝘼𝙣𝙙𝙐𝙥𝙙𝙖𝙩𝙚 𝙞𝙨 𝙖𝙩𝙤𝙢𝙞𝙘 𝙖𝙩 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙙𝙤𝙘𝙪𝙢𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙡𝙚𝙫𝙚𝙡
3️⃣ 𝙏𝙝𝙚 𝙣𝙪𝙡𝙡 𝙘𝙝𝙚𝙘𝙠 𝙞𝙨 𝙮𝙤𝙪𝙧 𝙚𝙧𝙧𝙤𝙧 𝙗𝙤𝙪𝙣𝙙𝙖𝙧𝙮
4️⃣ 𝙎𝙤𝙘𝙠𝙚𝙩.𝙞𝙤 𝙗𝙧𝙤𝙖𝙙𝙘𝙖𝙨𝙩𝙨 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙡𝙤𝙘𝙠 𝙞𝙢𝙢𝙚𝙙𝙞𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙡𝙮
𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗶𝗴𝗴𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗹𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗻
The fix isn't just about MongoDB. It's a mindset shift: never trust a read before a write in a concurrent system. Push your guard logic into the database operation itself, where it's atomic.
𝐻𝑎𝑣𝑒 𝑦𝑜𝑢 𝑟𝑢𝑛 𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑜 𝑟𝑎𝑐𝑒 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑑𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠 𝑖𝑛 𝑦𝑜𝑢𝑟 𝑜𝑤𝑛 𝑝𝑟𝑜𝑗𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑠? 𝐼'𝑑 𝑙𝑜𝑣𝑒 𝑡𝑜 ℎ𝑒𝑎𝑟 ℎ𝑜𝑤 𝑦𝑜𝑢 ℎ𝑎𝑛𝑑𝑙𝑒𝑑 𝑖𝑡.

zh.title: 我如何修复了一个实时座位预订系统中的竞态条件（为此失眠）
zh.summary: 座位预订系统中的竞态条件暴露了一个关键的并发缺陷：读-写操作永远不是原子性的。修复方案将读取和预订操作合并为单个原子MongoDB操作，Socket.io广播确保即时的客户端一致性。

fr.title: Comment j'ai corrigé une condition de course dans un système de réservation de places en direct (et j'ai perdu le sommeil à cause de cela)
fr.summary: Une condition de course dans un système de réservation de places a exposé un défaut de concurrence critique : les opérations lecture-puis-écriture ne sont jamais atomiques. La correction fusionne la lecture et la réservation en une seule opération MongoDB atomique, les diffusions Socket.io garantissant une cohérence côté client instantanée.

de.title: Wie ich eine Race Condition in einem Live-Platzbuchungssystem behoben habe (und dabei den Schlaf verlor)
de.summary: Eine Race Condition in einem Platzbuchungssystem offenbarte einen kritischen Concurrency-Fehler: Lese-Schreib-Operationen sind niemals atomar. Die Behebung vereinigt Lesen und Buchen in einer einzigen atomaren MongoDB-Operation, wobei Socket.io-Übertragungen eine sofortige Client-seitige Konsistenz gewährleisten.

es.title: Cómo arreglé una condición de carrera en un sistema de reserva de asientos en vivo (y perdí el sueño por ello)
es.summary: Una condición de carrera en un sistema de reserva de asientos expuso un defecto crítico de concurrencia: las operaciones de lectura-escritura nunca son atómicas. La solución colapsa la lectura y la reserva en una única operación MongoDB atómica, siendo las transmisiones Socket.io las que garantizan la consistencia instantánea del lado del cliente.

title: Linting and Formatting Python 2 Code in 2026: Navigating Obsolescence and Jython Constraints
summary: Python 2 is unmaintainable in 2026—officially unsupported since 2020 with zero modern tooling. Modern linters and formatters cannot parse Python 2 syntax, leaving developers with legacy Jython systems stranded without effective linting, formatting, or security scanning.
content:
Introduction: The Python 2 Dilemma in 2026


In 2026, Python 2 is not just obsolete—it’s a relic. Officially unsupported since 2020, its ecosystem has withered, leaving developers stranded with legacy systems that cannot be easily migrated. The problem is compounded by Jython’s rigid dependency on Python 2, a constraint that forces developers to maintain code in a language that modern tools have largely abandoned. This isn’t just about nostalgia for an older version; it’s about systems that, for regulatory, financial, or operational reasons, cannot be upgraded. The stakes are clear: without effective linting and formatting, Python 2 codebases degrade, introducing security vulnerabilities, performance bottlenecks, and maintenance nightmares.
Jython’s architecture is the root of the problem. It compiles Python 2 code to Java bytecode, a process that locks developers into Python 2 syntax and semantics. Attempting to run Python 3 code through Jython would trigger syntax errors (e.g., print() vs. print) and runtime failures due to incompatible standard library calls. Even if Python 3 tooling were available, Jython’s interpreter would reject the output, breaking the deployment pipeline. This isn’t a software incompatibility—it’s a mechanical mismatch between the Python 2 engine Jython expects and the Python 3 syntax modern tools produce.
Tools like Black, isort, and Flake8 have ceased Python 2 support. Black, for instance, dropped Python 2 compatibility in 2019, its parser now physically unable to process Python 2 syntax (e.g., urllib vs. urllib.parse). Flake8’s plugins, such as flake8-bugbear, rely on Python 3 AST structures, causing internal errors when encountering Python 2 code. Even if installed in a Python 2 environment, these tools would fail to execute due to missing dependencies like typed\_ast, which itself no longer supports Python 2. The result? A causal chain of failures: incompatible parser → AST mismatch → tool crash.
Given these constraints, developers mus

zh.title: 2026年Python 2代码的检查和格式化：应对过时和Jython限制
zh.summary: Python 2在2026年无法维护——自2020年以来官方不再支持，现代工具零支持。现代代码检查器和格式化工具无法解析Python 2语法，使得拥有遗留Jython系统的开发人员陷入困境，无法进行有效的代码检查、格式化或安全扫描。

fr.title: Linting et formatage du code Python 2 en 2026 : Naviguer dans l'obsolescence et les contraintes de Jython
fr.summary: Python 2 est impossible à maintenir en 2026—officiellement non supporté depuis 2020 sans aucun outillage moderne. Les linters et formateurs modernes ne peuvent pas analyser la syntaxe Python 2, laissant les développeurs avec des systèmes Jython hérités bloqués sans linting, formatage ou analyse de sécurité efficaces.

de.title: Linting und Formatierung von Python-2-Code im Jahr 2026: Navigation durch Obsoleszenz und Jython-Beschränkungen
de.summary: Python 2 ist 2026 nicht wartbar—seit 2020 offiziell nicht unterstützt und ohne modernes Tooling. Moderne Linter und Formatierer können Python-2-Syntax nicht analysieren, wodurch Entwickler mit veralteten Jython-Systemen ohne effektives Linting, Formatierung oder Sicherheitsprüfung hängengelassen werden.

es.title: Linting y formateo de código Python 2 en 2026: Navegando por la obsolescencia y las limitaciones de Jython
es.summary: Python 2 es inmantenible en 2026—oficialmente no soportado desde 2020 sin herramientas modernas. Los linters y formateadores modernos no pueden analizar la sintaxis de Python 2, dejando a los desarrolladores con sistemas Jython heredados sin linting, formateo o escaneo de seguridad efectivo.

title: Code Mode: Batching MCP Tool Calls in a WASM Sandbox to Cut LLM Token Usage by 30-80%
summary: Code Mode batches multiple LLM tool calls into a single WASM-sandboxed JavaScript execution, eliminating expensive round-trips and reducing token usage by 30–80%. VoidLLM's self-hosted implementation integrates with any MCP server without external dependencies.
content:
The Problem: One Tool Call Per Turn Is Expensive


If you've worked with LLMs and tool use, you know the pattern. The model decides it needs to call a tool. It emits a tool call. Your system executes it, returns the result. The model reads the result, reasons about it, and decides it needs another tool call. Repeat.
Every round trip burns tokens. The model re-reads the entire conversation history each time. For workflows that touch 5-10 tools — think "look up the customer, check their subscription, fetch recent invoices, calculate usage, draft a summary" — you're paying for the same context window over and over. The token cost adds up fast, and latency compounds with each turn.
Code Mode flips the pattern. Instead of one tool call per LLM turn, the model writes a short JavaScript program that orchestrates multiple tool calls in a single execution. The model gets the results all at once and reasons over the complete picture.
This is inspired by Cloudflare's Code Mode concept. The difference: VoidLLM's implementation is fully self-hosted, runs in a WASM-sandboxed runtime, and integrates with any MCP server you're already running.
In practice, this reduces token usage by 30-80% depending on the complexity of the tool workflow.
The execution pipeline:
The LLM receives auto-generated TypeScript type declarations describing all available MCP tools
The LLM emits a JavaScript block that calls the tools it needs
VoidLLM executes the JS inside a QuickJS runtime compiled to WebAssembly

Tool calls within the JS are dispatched to upstream MCP servers via Streamable HTTP
Results are collected and returned to the LLM in a single response
The WASM layer is powered by Wazero, a pure Go WebAssembly runtime. No CGO, no external dependencies. VoidLLM stays a single static binary.
Tools are exposed through an ES6 Proxy pattern — the LLM can call any tool by name without per-tool bindings.
const [customer, invoices] = await Promise.all([
  tools.crm.get_customer({ id: "cust_8a3f" }),

zh.title: 代码模式：在WASM沙箱中批处理MCP工具调用，将LLM令牌使用量减少30-80%
zh.summary: 代码模式将多个LLM工具调用批处理为单个WASM沙箱化的JavaScript执行，消除了昂贵的往返行程，将令牌使用量减少了30-80%。VoidLLM的自托管实现与任何MCP服务器集成，无需外部依赖。

fr.title: Mode Code : Batching d'appels d'outils MCP dans un bac à sable WASM pour réduire l'utilisation de jetons LLM de 30-80%
fr.summary: Le Mode Code regroupe plusieurs appels d'outils LLM en une seule exécution JavaScript isolée en WASM, éliminant les allers-retours coûteux et réduisant l'utilisation de jetons de 30–80%. L'implémentation auto-hébergée de VoidLLM s'intègre à n'importe quel serveur MCP sans dépendances externes.

de.title: Code-Modus: Batching von MCP-Tool-Aufrufen in einer WASM-Sandbox zur Reduzierung der LLM-Token-Nutzung um 30-80%
de.summary: Code-Modus gruppiert mehrere LLM-Tool-Aufrufe in eine einzige WASM-isolierte JavaScript-Ausführung, eliminiert teure Hin- und Rückfahrten und reduziert die Token-Nutzung um 30–80%. Die selbstgehostete Implementierung von VoidLLM lässt sich ohne externe Abhängigkeiten in jeden MCP-Server integrieren.

es.title: Modo Código: Agrupamiento de llamadas de herramientas MCP en una caja de arena WASM para reducir el uso de tokens de LLM en un 30-80%
es.summary: El Modo Código agrupa múltiples llamadas de herramientas LLM en una única ejecución JavaScript aislada en WASM, eliminando costosos viajes de ida y vuelta y reduciendo el uso de tokens en un 30–80%. La implementación autohospedada de VoidLLM se integra con cualquier servidor MCP sin dependencias externas.

title: Rails vs Laravel vs Django vs NestJS: Why I Still Choose Ruby
summary: Rails remains the fastest framework for shipping products. NestJS buries business logic in boilerplate dependency injection, Django is overly explicit, and Laravel borrows Rails' ideas but lacks its elegance—Ruby's simplicity wins.

zh.title: Rails vs Laravel vs Django vs NestJS：为什么我仍然选择Ruby
zh.summary: Rails仍然是最快的产品交付框架。NestJS在样板依赖注入中隐藏业务逻辑，Django过于显式，Laravel借用Rails的想法但缺乏其优雅性——Ruby的简洁性获胜。

fr.title: Rails vs Laravel vs Django vs NestJS : Pourquoi je choisis toujours Ruby
fr.summary: Rails reste le framework le plus rapide pour livrer des produits. NestJS enterre la logique métier dans l'injection de dépendances boilerplate, Django est trop explicite, et Laravel emprunte les idées de Rails mais lui manque son élégance—la simplicité de Ruby gagne.

de.title: Rails vs Laravel vs Django vs NestJS: Warum ich immer noch Ruby wähle
de.summary: Rails bleibt das schnellste Framework für die Produktlieferung. NestJS vergräbt Geschäftslogik in Boilerplate-Abhängigkeitsinjektion, Django ist zu explizit, und Laravel leiht Rails' Ideen aus, aber es fehlt die Eleganz—Rubys Einfachheit gewinnt.

es.title: Rails vs Laravel vs Django vs NestJS: Por qué sigo eligiendo Ruby
es.summary: Rails sigue siendo el framework más rápido para entregar productos. NestJS entierra la lógica empresarial en inyección de dependencias de plantilla, Django es demasiado explícito, y Laravel toma prestadas las ideas de Rails pero carece de su elegancia—la simplicidad de Ruby gana.

title: Why I Chose Remotion + FFmpeg for Server-Side Video Rendering
summary: Server-side video rendering with Remotion and FFmpeg eliminates client-side performance variability and hardware dependency. Offloading rendering to the server guarantees consistent, high-quality output across all users and resolutions.
content:
Building a video creation platform, the "Video Studio," presented a significant technical challenge: how to enable users to generate high-quality videos directly from the platform. This required a robust and scalable solution for server-side video rendering, capable of handling various resolutions and quality presets. This article details the journey, the challenges, the chosen approach using Remotion and FFmpeg on a Railway backend, and the resulting performance and cost metrics.
The primary hurdle was providing users with the ability to render videos in different resolutions (1080p, 4K, and 8K) and quality settings (Draft, Standard, High, and Ultra) without impacting the user experience. This meant the rendering process had to be fast, reliable, and cost-effective.
Initial attempts at client-side rendering proved inadequate. Client-side rendering, where the user's browser handles the video generation, faced several limitations:
  Performance Bottlenecks: The user's hardware (CPU, GPU, and RAM) directly impacts rendering speed. Complex compositions or high-resolution videos could lead to slow rendering times, freezing, and a poor user experience.
  Hardware Variability: The performance of client-side rendering varies significantly based on the user's device. This inconsistency makes it difficult to guarantee a consistent rendering experience across all users.
  Limited Capabilities: Client-side rendering often lacks the processing power to handle complex video compositions, advanced effects, and high-resolution outputs efficiently.
These limitations made client-side rendering unsuitable for a platform aiming to provide professional-quality video creation tools.
Server-side rendering (SSR) emerged as the clear solution to these challenges. SSR offloads the computationally intensive video rendering tasks to the server, freeing up the user's device and ensuring consistent performance regardless of the user's hardware. This approach offered several key advantages:
  Co

zh.title: 为什么我选择Remotion + FFmpeg进行服务器端视频渲染
zh.summary: 使用Remotion和FFmpeg进行服务器端视频渲染消除了客户端性能变异和硬件依赖。将渲染卸载到服务器可保证跨所有用户和分辨率的一致、高质量输出。

fr.title: Pourquoi j'ai choisi Remotion + FFmpeg pour le rendu vidéo côté serveur
fr.summary: Le rendu vidéo côté serveur avec Remotion et FFmpeg élimine la variabilité des performances côté client et la dépendance matérielle. Décharger le rendu sur le serveur garantit une sortie cohérente et de haute qualité sur tous les utilisateurs et résolutions.

de.title: Warum ich Remotion + FFmpeg für Server-seitiges Video-Rendering gewählt habe
de.summary: Video-Rendering auf der Serverseite mit Remotion und FFmpeg beseitigt Leistungsvariabilität und Hardware-Abhängigkeit auf der Clientseite. Das Offloaden des Renderings auf den Server garantiert konsistente, hochwertige Ausgaben für alle Benutzer und Auflösungen.

es.title: Por qué elegí Remotion + FFmpeg para renderización de video del lado del servidor
es.summary: La renderización de video del lado del servidor con Remotion y FFmpeg elimina la variabilidad del rendimiento del lado del cliente y la dependencia del hardware. Descargar el renderizado al servidor garantiza una salida consistente y de alta calidad en todos los usuarios y resoluciones.

title: Next.js 16 Multi-Tenancy: Organizations, Team Invites, and Per-Org Billing
summary: Multi-tenancy in Next.js 16 requires organizations as the primary container—not users. Using Prisma to model orgs, members, and invites, plus middleware for context injection and Stripe for per-org billing, creates the foundation for collaborative SaaS.
content:
Next.js 16 Multi-Tenancy: Organizations, Team Invites, and Per-Org Billing


Multi-tenancy is the backbone of any serious SaaS product. When users need to collaborate under a shared workspace — invite teammates, manage billing together, and work on shared data — you need a clean organizational layer beneath your auth system. This tutorial walks through building exactly that with Next.js 16, Prisma, and Stripe.
By the end, you'll have:
A Prisma schema supporting orgs, memberships, and roles
Middleware that injects org context into every request
A team invite flow via email
Per-org Stripe subscriptions
First, the data model. Organizations sit between users and resources. A user can belong to many orgs, each org has members with roles, and billing attaches to the org — not the individual user.
// prisma/schema.prisma

model Organization {
  id                String       @id @default(cuid())
  name              String
  slug              String       @unique
  stripeCustomerId  String?      @unique
  stripeSubId       String?
  plan              String       @default("free")
  createdAt         DateTime     @default(now())
  members           Member[]
  invites           Invite[]
}

model Member {
  id        String       @id @default(cuid())
  orgId     String
  userId    String
  role      Role         @default(MEMBER)
  createdAt DateTime     @default(now())
  org       Organization @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  user      User         @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([orgId, userId])
}

model Invite {
  id        String       @id @default(cuid())
  orgId     String
  email     String
  role      Role         @default(MEMBER)
  token     String       @unique @default(cuid())
  expiresAt DateTime
  accepted  Boolean      @default(false)
  org       Organization @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

enum Role {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  MEMBER
}

Run npx prisma migrate

zh.title: Next.js 16多租户：组织、团队邀请和按组织计费
zh.summary: Next.js 16中的多租户需要组织作为主容器——而不是用户。使用Prisma建模组织、成员和邀请，加上用于上下文注入的中间件和用于按组织计费的Stripe，为协作SaaS奠定基础。

fr.summary: La multi-location dans Next.js 16 nécessite des organisations comme conteneur principal—pas des utilisateurs. L'utilisation de Prisma pour modéliser les organisations, les membres et les invitations, plus les middleware pour l'injection de contexte et Stripe pour la facturation par organisation, crée la base d'un SaaS collaboratif.

de.summary: Multi-Mandantenfähigkeit in Next.js 16 erfordert Organisationen als primären Container—nicht Benutzer. Die Verwendung von Prisma zur Modellierung von Organisationen, Mitgliedern und Einladungen, plus Middleware zur Kontextinjektion und Stripe zur Pro-Org-Abrechnung, schafft die Grundlage für kollaboratives SaaS.

es.title: Next.js 16 Multi-Tenancy: Organizaciones, invitaciones de equipo y facturación por organización
es.summary: El multi-tenancy en Next.js 16 requiere organizaciones como contenedor principal—no usuarios. Usar Prisma para modelar organizaciones, miembros e invitaciones, más middleware para inyección de contexto y Stripe para facturación por organización, crea la base para SaaS colaborativo.

title: WriteRight — AI Writing Coach for K–8 Teachers, Built with Notion MCP
summary: WriteRight analyzes student writing against Common Core standards in seconds, identifying instructional gaps and smart groupings that would take teachers 3–4 hours per unit. Notion MCP enables agentic, schema-flexible reading of student samples at scale.
content:
This is a submission for the Notion MCP Challenge
WriteRight is an AI-powered writing analysis tool that helps K–8 teachers give differentiated feedback to every student — without spending hours reading and planning.
Teachers store student writing samples in a Notion database. WriteRight reads them all via Notion MCP, analyzes each one against Common Core State Standards (CCSS) for the student's grade level, and generates:
Per-student teaching points (2–3 next-step instructional moves)
Strengths (what the student is doing well, tied to standards)
Standards addressed (e.g. W.3.1, L.3.2)
Smart small groups — students clustered by shared instructional need, with a suggested activity
The full analysis saves back to Notion in one click.
Live app: https://write-right-app.web.app
GitHub: https://github.com/watts4/write-right
A third-grade teacher with 28 students has 28 different writing stages. Reviewing every sample, finding the right CCSS standard, planning differentiated groups — that's 3–4 hours of prep per unit. WriteRight does it in under 2 minutes.
  
  



  
  
  Show us the code


GitHub: https://github.com/watts4/write-right
The repo includes:
React 18 / TypeScript / Vite / Tailwind frontend (Firebase Hosting)
Node.js / Express / TypeScript backend (Google Cloud Run)
Notion OAuth 2.0 + PKCE flow
MCP agentic read loop with parallel tool execution
CCSS Writing + Language standards K–8
Seed script for mock Grade 3 class (8 students with deliberate skill gaps)
Notion MCP is the core reading engine of WriteRight — not a thin wrapper, but the actual mechanism that makes agentic, schema-flexible reading of student writing possible.
Notion DB ──► Phase 1: MCP Agentic Read
                   │
                   ▼
             Phase 2: Claude Analysis (CCSS-anchored JSON)
                   │
                   ▼
           UI Results ──► /api/save ──► Notion (REST write-back)

The backend spawns @notionhq/notion-mcp-server (the official npm package) as a subprocess on

zh.title: WriteRight——用Notion MCP构建的K-8教师AI写作教练
zh.summary: WriteRight根据共同核心标准在几秒内分析学生写作，识别教学差距和智能分组，通常需要教师每个单元花费3-4小时。Notion MCP使能够灵活地大规模阅读学生样本。

fr.summary: WriteRight analyse les écrits des étudiants selon les normes Common Core en quelques secondes, identifiant les lacunes pédagogiques et les regroupements intelligents qui prendraient 3 à 4 heures par unité aux enseignants. Notion MCP permet une lecture agentique et flexible des schémas des échantillons étudiants à l'échelle.

Notion MCP ermöglicht agentisches, schemaflexibles Lesen von Schülerstichproben im großen Maßstab.

Notion MCP permite la lectura ágil y flexible de esquemas de muestras de estudiantes a escala.

title: How is DNS resolver happening?
summary: DNS resolution translates domain names to IP addresses through a cascading query to root, TLD, and authoritative name servers. Browsers and system caches shorten lookup times, making the internet functional by hiding complexity from users.
content:
Whenever we enter a website’s name like www.wikipedia.com in a browser’s address bar as my sir said as a example, the website opens in front of us in a matter of seconds. But behind this simple operation, there is a complex operation going on in the background known as DNS resolution. Here we will learn about it in detail.
What is DNS?
Step-By-Step DNS Resolution Process
Step 1: User Request
The process starts with a user request. When a user enters a domain name in a web browser’s address bar, this process starts.
Step 2: Browser and System Cache
The web browser checks if it already knows this IP address or not. If it is stored in its cache or system cache, it directly uses it.
Step 3: DNS Resolver
If it is not stored in cache, it sends a request to a DNS resolver. This resolver is provided by an Internet Service Provider (ISP). This resolver acts as a mediator and finds the IP address correctly.
Step 4: Root Server
The DNS resolver sends a request to the root DNS server. The root server is not aware of the IP address but knows where to direct the resolver to obtain the required information.
Step 5: TLD Server
The Top-Level Domain server is responsible for handling domain extensions like .com, .org, or .net. This server directs the resolver to the required domain server.
Step 6: Authoritative Name Server
The authoritative name server is aware of the IP address associated with the domain. This server provides the IP address to the DNS resolver.
Step 7: Returning the IP Address
The IP address is sent back to the DNS resolver, which forwards it to the browser.
Step 8: Connecting to the Server
The browser sends a request to the server using the IP address received in the previous steps.
Why DNS Resolution is Important
It makes the internet user-friendly
It allows quick and efficient access to websites
It reduces the need to remember complex IP addresses
Conclusion
DNS resolution is an essential process that occurs every time we access a website. The process involves a

zh.title: DNS解析器是如何工作的?
zh.summary: DNS解析通过对根服务器、TLD服务器和权威名称服务器的级联查询，将域名转换为IP地址。浏览器和系统缓存缩短查找时间，通过对用户隐藏复杂性使互联网正常运行。

fr.title: Comment fonctionne la résolution DNS?
fr.summary: La résolution DNS traduit les noms de domaine en adresses IP via une requête en cascade aux serveurs racines, TLD et serveurs de noms faisant autorité. Les caches du navigateur et du système raccourcissent les temps de recherche, rendant Internet fonctionnel en cachant la complexité aux utilisateurs.

de.title: Wie funktioniert DNS-Auflösung?
de.summary: Die DNS-Auflösung übersetzt Domänennamen in IP-Adressen durch kaskadierende Abfragen an Root-, TLD- und autorative Nameserver. Browser- und Systemcaches verkürzen die Lookup-Zeiten und machen das Internet funktional, indem sie die Komplexität vor Benutzern verbergen.

es.title: ¿Cómo funciona la resolución DNS?
es.summary: La resolución DNS traduce nombres de dominio a direcciones IP a través de consultas en cascada a servidores raíz, TLD y servidores de nombres autorizados. Los cachés del navegador y del sistema acortan los tiempos de búsqueda, haciendo funcional Internet al ocultar la complejidad a los usuarios.

title: 7 Enrichment Tips to Make Your Cat's Life as Happy as a Gift: The Ultimate Guide
summary: Indoor cats' behavioral problems stem from repressed hunting drives—the five-stage hunting sequence must be completed or stress accumulates. Strategic environmental enrichment redesigns living spaces to stimulate instincts and prevent destructive behaviors.
content:
The Ultimate Guide to Feline Environmental Enrichment: Live Happily Like You Got a 7th Room


> ⚠️ Medical Disclaimer: This content is for general informational purposes only and does not replace professional veterinary diagnosis or treatment. If you suspect your cat has a health issue, please consult a veterinarian immediately.
Have you ever wondered why your cat is scratching furniture at 3 AM, staring blankly at a wall, or suddenly becoming aggressive with no apparent trigger? You might think they are just being "bad." But often, the root cause isn't a behavioral problem—it's an environmental one.
When cats indoors feel bored or experience repetitive behaviors, simply swapping out toys often isn't enough. The solution lies in Environmental Enrichment: a strategic redesign of their living space to stimulate their natural instincts.
This guide is brought to you by PlayCat Research (playcat.xyz), a Korean behavioral enrichment project dedicated to understanding the feline mind. We believe that by applying principles of feline ethology, you can drastically reduce stress, fulfill natural instincts, and create a home where your cat lives as happily as if they had a "7th room" to explore.
To stop the unwanted behaviors, we must first understand the psychological drivers behind them. Cats are not just pets; they are complex predators with deep-seated evolutionary needs.
Cats living indoors never leave the house, yet their innate hunting drive remains active. According to animal behavior studies, cats release dopamine and feel happiness by completing the Hunting Sequence. This sequence consists of five distinct stages:
 Observation

 Stalking

 Chasing

 Capturing

 Killing and Evisceration (post-hunt processing)
If any of these steps are missing, the cat perceives the experience as a "failed hunt," leading to frustration, anxiety, and psychological imbalance. This repression often manifests as problem behaviors like excessive scratching, chewing, or loud vocalizations.
T

zh.title: 7个丰富技巧让你的猫的生活像礼物一样幸福：终极指南
zh.summary: 室内猫的行为问题源于压抑的狩猎欲望——五阶段狩猎序列必须完成，否则压力会积累。战略性环境丰富重新设计生活空间以刺激本能并防止破坏性行为。

fr.title: 7 conseils d'enrichissement pour rendre la vie de votre chat aussi heureuse qu'un cadeau : Le guide ultime
fr.summary: Les problèmes comportementaux des chats d'intérieur proviennent de pulsions de chasse réprimées—la séquence de chasse en cinq étapes doit être complétée ou le stress s'accumule. L'enrichissement environnemental stratégique réaménage les espaces de vie pour stimuler les instincts et prévenir les comportements destructeurs.

de.title: 7 Bereicherungstipps, um das Leben Ihrer Katze so glücklich wie ein Geschenk zu machen: Der ultimative Leitfaden
de.summary: Verhaltensproaleme bei Hauskatzen stammen von unterdrückten Jagdtrieben—die fünfstufige Jagdsequenz muss abgeschlossen sein oder Stress staut sich auf. Strategische Umweltbereichung gestaltet Wohnräume um, um Instinkte zu stimulieren und destruktives Verhalten zu verhindern.

es.title: 7 consejos de enriquecimiento para hacer que la vida de tu gato sea tan feliz como un regalo: La guía definitiva
es.summary: Los problemas de comportamiento de los gatos de interior provienen de impulsos de caza reprimidos—la secuencia de caza de cinco etapas debe completarse o el estrés se acumula. El enriquecimiento ambiental estratégico rediseña los espacios de vida para estimular los instintos y prevenir comportamientos destructivos.
